title: Keep track of any build-args used during buildah bud --layers
body:
Add any build-args used in the build process to the layers history
for future comparison when building with --layers.

Fixes https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1391

Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: If this PR treats `--build-arg` and plain `ARG` similarly, it maybe also solves #1507.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 07950be) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

> @umohnani8: @h-vetinari I will look into #1507 :)

Cool! :)
Maybe also tangentially related: #1503

Code looks fine, but I've spotted a slight difference between Docker and this that we probably ought to touch up.  If you do:
```
# cat ~/Dockerfile.withuser
FROM busybox
ARG user
ARG user2
RUN echo $user
RUN echo $user2
ENV MYBAD=bad

buildah bud --layers -t tom -f ~/Dockerfile.withuser --build-arg user=0 --build-arg user2=3 .
docker build -t tom -f ~/Dockerfile.withuser --build-arg user=1 --build-arg user2=2 .
```

Buildah history shows:
```
buildah inspect tom
...
      {
            "created": "2019-04-17T18:19:15.705509758Z",
            "created_by": "user=0 user2=3 /bin/sh -c echo $user"
        },
        {
            "created": "2019-04-17T18:19:16.844597882Z",
            "created_by": "user=0 user2=3 /bin/sh -c echo $user2"
        },

```

Docker history shows:
```
IMAGE               CREATED             CREATED BY                                      SIZE                COMMENT
420c228b495e        5 seconds ago       /bin/sh -c #(nop)  ENV MYBAD=bad                0B                  
14c4e5a79540        5 seconds ago       |2 user2=2 user=1 /bin/sh -c echo $user2        0B                  
52affc18d01f        6 seconds ago       |2 user2=2 user=1 /bin/sh -c echo $user         0B                  
ce34f4d6e0c1        7 seconds ago       /bin/sh -c #(nop)  ARG user2                    0B                  
9480e5de1551        8 days ago          /bin/sh -c #(nop)  ARG user                     0B                  
af2f74c517aa        2 weeks ago         /bin/sh -c #(nop)  CMD ["sh"]                   0B                  
<missing>           2 weeks ago         /bin/sh -c #(nop) ADD file:6051b0ebe4098ccbb…   1.2MB        
```
not the pipe 2 at the start of the echo history commands.  When I ran it with one build-arg it was "|1" and with 3 args "|3".  Could we add that?

What's of head scratching interest is for the 2 args case they switched the order of the  build-args in the history.  For 3 args, they looked to have moved that first argument to the last position and then kept the others in the order received.

```
5f3d788341bd        5 seconds ago       |3 user2=2 user3=3 user=1 /bin/sh -c echo $u…   0B                  

```

This is with Docker Version:           18.09.0

IDK for sure if we need to change the args order in the history to match, but we probably ought to add the pipe and number of args if possible.

Other than that, really nice piece of code craftsmanship here @umohnani8
@TomSweeneyRedHat thanks for testing it! And great catch, I will rework it to include the pipe and number of args :)
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 391a5be) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
